data.reasoningText:
Now let me build the project to see the actual error, and I can already see the issue. The `GenerateSampleCode` target generates a `.cs` file into the intermediate output path, but it doesn't add the generated file to the `Compile` item group. So the file gets written to disk but the compiler never sees it.

The fix is to add an `<ItemGroup>` within the target that includes the generated file in the `Compile` items:

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
</ItemGroup>
```

Let me also fix the path separator - on Linux, `\` won't work. The `.csproj` uses backslash `\` for path separators in `_GeneratedCodeDir`, which won't work on Linux.


I should also check the `AssemblyFile` attribute in `UsingTask` since it's using backslashes too — `$(MSBuildToolsPath)\Microsoft.Build.Tasks.Core.dll` could be problematic on Linux as well. Let me build and see what errors come up.

data.content:
The file is generated successfully but never added to the compilation. The `GenerateSampleCode` target writes the file to disk but doesn't include it in the `Compile` item group, so the C# compiler never sees it.

**Fix:** Add a `<Compile Include>` for the generated file in the target.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The file is generated (`Generated code file: obj/Debug/net8.0/Generated/GeneratedInfo.cs`) but the compiler can't find `TestProject.Generated` namespace because the generated file isn't added to the `Compile` item group.

The fix is to add the generated file to `<Compile>` in the target, after writing it.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.content:
**Root cause:** The `GenerateSampleCode` target wrote `GeneratedInfo.cs` to the intermediate output directory but never added it to the `Compile` item group. The compiler had no knowledge of the file.

**Fix:** Added `<Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />` inside an `<ItemGroup>` within the target, so the generated file is included in compilation after being written.
